title: About
text:
I’m an engineer who loves building, learning, and solving complex problems. I’m driven by curiosity rather than allegiance to any single discipline—electronics, machine learning, biology, physics—whatever the problem demands.
I hold a degree in Electronics and Communication Engineering. My early work on autonomous robots gave me a foundation in systems thinking that I now bring to AI.

title: Welcome to My Blog
text:
Hello! Welcome to my personal blog. This is where I’ll be sharing my thoughts, learnings, and insights about:

Machine Learning & AI: Latest developments, techniques, and applications
Engineering: Problem-solving approaches and technical solutions
Career & Growth: Lessons learned and experiences in tech
Life & Learning: Personal reflections and continuous learning journey

I believe in keeping things simple and focused. This blog is my digital notebook where I capture ideas, document learning, and share what I discover along the way.


I’ll be writing about:

Technical deep-dives into ML/AI topics
Career insights and lessons learned
Tools and techniques I find useful
Thoughts on the future of technology




Feel free to reach out to me on Twitter or LinkedIn if you’d like to discuss any of these topics!
Happy reading! 🚀

title: Ayush Thakur
text:
I’m an engineer driven by curiosity—exploring machine learning, AI systems, and the hard problems that sit at their intersection.
I work as Manager, AI Engineer at Weights & Biases (now part of CoreWeave), leading open-source integrations that help ML practitioners build complete MLOps stacks. Over five years at W&B, I’ve specialized in LLM applications and evaluation, created courses, and written extensively about what I’ve learned.
I’m a Google Developer Expert in Machine Learning and a Kaggle Notebooks Master.
When I’m not working, I watch anime, think about hard problems, and occasionally 3D print things.

title: Writing
text:
Technical articles and tutorials I’ve written on the W&B platform—covering deep learning, computer vision, LLMs, and practical ML engineering.

Understanding Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF): Part 1

How to Evaluate, Compare, and Optimize LLM Systems

Train Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) With Limited Data
In-Domain GAN Inversion for Real Image Editing

Image Segmentation Using Keras and Weights & Biases
Object Localization With Keras and Weights & Biases

Integrating Keras with Weights & Biases

How to Prevent TensorFlow From Fully Allocating GPU Memory
LSTM RNN in Keras: Examples of One-to-Many, Many-to-One & Many-to-Many

HuggingFace & Transformers

How To Fine-Tune Hugging Face Transformers on a Custom Dataset
Examples of Early Stopping in HuggingFace Transformers
SimpleTransformers: Transformers Made Easy
Taming Transformers for High-Resolution Image Synthesis

Deep Learning Fundamentals

What’s the Optimal Batch Size to Train a Neural Network?
